mentions.note: Matched SSS at 4.1%, small revenue beat, inline earnings +8% YoY; declined to raise FY guidance citing higher fuel and consumer pressure. Stock tumbled 7.2% despite solid execution; valuation sky-high relative to growth; pullback may present buying opportunity.
Revamped into great company for all income levels; excellent natural food section; terrific numbers expected; often overlooked by snobs.
Holds price line vs. inflation; won't disappoint customers; among retail winners rallying today as inflation pushes consumers toward discount retailers.
Finally hit after big pre-war rally; consumer weakness showing.
Incredible run; consolidating terrifically; retail strength; good defensive quality with growth characteristics.
Trading at 42x FY2027 earnings, near all-time rich multiple; 3.4% decline signals stock price may be too high for consumers; juggernaut momentum stalling.
Barely up; retail sector failing to lead despite expectations.
Safe dividend play; bounced off support, ready to run; Cramer bullish for income-focused, defensive positioning in weak market.
THE stock to buy in this difficult market environment alongside Costco; represents difficult economic conditions; staple play for bear market; liked equally to Costco.
Consumer/retail stocks rallied on war-resolution hopes; Cramer cited Walmart as exemplar of AI-driven productivity (coding, merchandising, consumer search) enabling revenue growth.
Up 8% YTD; high price multiple justified as slowdown hedge, but stock not cheap—hold for protective positioning if rate cuts come.
80% of march to $1T valuation from multiple expansion, not earnings; added experience + convenience to price leadership; MVP with high purchase intent; 5–8% faster growth profile.
Value retailer selling cheaper than competition; understandable business model; reporting tomorrow; benefits from rotation to tangible, in-demand businesses.
Up 4% with new CEO; retailers reach for stocks on strong economic data; institutional buying amid PMI strength.
Retail jailbreak includes Walmart; benefiting from lower-rate environment and consumer sentiment.
